billing.rule: A customer may have at most one active subscription per product family.
An invoice's line-item amounts must sum exactly to its total.
A trialing subscription becomes active only after its first successful payment.
An active subscription whose latest invoice is unpaid past the grace period must not remain active.
A canceled subscription never transitions to any other state.
Total refunds for an invoice may never exceed the amount captured for it.
Every invoice references an existing subscription.
The same billing period is never invoiced twice for one subscription.
Available plus reserved balance always equals the account's total balance.
After the configured maximum failed payment retries, a subscription is marked delinquent and no further retries occur.

revrec.rule: For every performance obligation, recognized plus deferred revenue equals its allocated contract value.
Cumulative recognized revenue for an obligation never decreases.
A ratable obligation recognizes an equal amount in every period of its service term.
No revenue entry may post to a closed accounting period.
A usage-based obligation recognizes revenue only in or after the period in which the usage was reported.
At most one accounting period is open at any time.
Every revenue entry references an existing obligation and an existing accounting period.
Recognized revenue for an obligation never exceeds its allocated amount.
A closed period's recognized total is immutable; late adjustments post as corrections to an open period.
An on-delivery obligation recognizes its full allocated amount in the period of delivery.
